title: Authorization, Object Ownership, Properties, Functions, and Tenancy · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective deny-by-default authorization decision matrix; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deny-by-default authorization decision matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.
Load or constrain the target within the authorized tenant and ownership relationship before read, write, delete, export, or nested access.
Allowlist readable and writable properties by operation and principal, reject server-owned fields, and prevent over-posting through explicit command types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Inspect the failure record · Debug Authz Subject Action Resource
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 2123 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce tenant, action, and property authorization as resource policy; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.
